archetype: ARCHETYPE-3 (Runtime/Behavior) — primary unit packages/plugin-sagas-core owns saga runtime ports + engine + bridge dedup; the KV durable adapter and HTTP wiring land in plugins/sagas. F-13 saga/runtime invariants apply.
scopeOverlays: SCOPE-service (plugins/sagas/services HTTP contract + composition root touched)
lockedDecisions: Durable backend = Deno KV via Deno.openKv (NETSCRIPT_SAGA_KV_PATH), mirroring KvTriggerIdempotencyStore — NOT Prisma/Postgres
KV adapters (KvSagaIdempotencyStore, KvSagaAppliedKeyStore, openSagaRuntimeKv) live in plugins/sagas/src/runtime, keeping packages/plugin-sagas-core JSR-clean (no Deno.openKv in published core)
New SagaAppliedKeyStore port added to core (interface only) with a real in-process MemorySagaAppliedKeyStore default — engine is never a silent no-op
Engine applied-key guard lives in SagaEngine.#handleEntry (so raw createSagaEngine consumers are also covered), returns alreadyApplied:true with no throw and no re-persist, per doctrine 08
Reuse the existing SagaIdempotencyPort/SagaIdempotencyReservation/SagaIdempotencyTarget contracts and formatIdempotencyKey (promoted to exported sagaIdempotencyKey) — no new transport-dedup interface
idempotencyKey added to PublishMessageInput contract (TS type + zod schema in lockstep) as optional; SagaMessage.idempotencyKey and native.idempotency seams already exist and are reused
Atomic first-wins reservation via kv.atomic().check({versionstamp:null}).set(...,{expireIn}); TTL default 24h matching DEFAULT_IDEMPOTENCY_TTL_MS
Composition roots wired: services/src/main.ts, saga-runner.ts, saga-supervisor.ts all inject durable idempotency + appliedKeys; main.ts header corrected (no false Prisma-durability claim)
No scaffold-output change → e2e:cli NOT in the gate set; aspire/version pins untouched; @netscript/cli unaffected
contracts: SagaAppliedKeyStore.recordApplied(instanceId, idempotencyKey): Promise<{ applied: boolean }> — NEW core port (saga-applied-key-port.ts), doctrine applied-keys store
SagaEngineOptions.appliedKeys?: SagaAppliedKeyStore + SagaEngineHandleResult.alreadyApplied: boolean — engine guard seam
KvSagaIdempotencyStore implements SagaIdempotencyPort + KvSagaAppliedKeyStore implements SagaAppliedKeyStore + openSagaRuntimeKv() — plugin-layer durable adapters
Exported sagaIdempotencyKey(target, key) helper promoted from private formatIdempotencyKey for adapter reuse
testPlan: unit (core): MemorySagaAppliedKeyStore.recordApplied returns applied=true first call, applied=false on duplicate (same instanceId+key)

unit (core): SagaEngine with appliedKeys — first publish with idempotencyKey runs handler + persists; second identical publish short-circuits (alreadyApplied=true), handler NOT re-invoked (spy count==1), #persistTransition NOT called again (mock store save count==1)
unit (core): SagaEngine without idempotencyKey behaves unchanged (no guard); raw createSagaEngine consumer path covered
unit (core): publish with distinct idempotencyKeys both apply; distinct instanceId+same key both apply (scope is per-instance)

integration (plugin, --unstable-kv): KvSagaIdempotencyStore.reserve — accepted=true first, accepted=false within TTL, accepted=true after TTL expiry; survives a fresh store over the same Deno.openKv path (durability/replica-share)
integration (plugin, --unstable-kv): KvSagaAppliedKeyStore.recordApplied atomic first-wins across two concurrent calls (only one applied=true)

integration (service): publishSagaMessage threads idempotencyKey from SagaPublishMessageInput → runtime.publish options and onto the SagaRuntimeMessage; contract zod parse accepts/round-trips idempotencyKey

failure-path: duplicate publish returns published:true with already-applied semantics (no thrown error, supervisor-safe); KV open failure surfaces a structured SagasError not a silent fallback to memory in the deployed root
wiring: createSagaRuntime forwards engineOptions.appliedKeys to the engine (constructed engine has the injected store, not the memory default) — assert via injected spy store
planGateSelfCheck: PASS expected on all 8 boxes. Research present & re-baselined against main tip cc3b8731 with corrected file:line for every gap (none already resolved). Decisions locked with rationale (KV-not-Prisma, plugin-layer adapter, engine-layer guard, reuse existing seams). Open-decision sweep: TTL default and applied-key TTL marked safe-to-defer (configurable, no rework). Commit slices: 9, ordered, each names proof+gate+files (<30). Risk register present with mitigations. Gate set selected from archetype-gate-matrix (Arch 3 + F-13 + SCOPE-service, e2e:cli justified-excluded). Deferred scope explicit (durable SagaStorePort = sibling slice). jsr-audit applied: KV adapters kept OUT of JSR-published core; publish:dry-run gate named; no slow-type/permission surface added to plugin-sagas-core public exports. Residual FAIL_PLAN risk: low — only if PLAN-EVAL insists the durable SagaStorePort be in-slice, but evidence + sibling slice scoping justify the boundary.
openQuestions: Applied-key KV TTL: unbounded vs long TTL (e.g. 7d). LOCKED default = unbounded (no expireIn) with a configurable activeTtlMs option; safe to defer, configurable, no rework.
Should bridge transport-dedup and engine applied-key share one KV namespace prefix? LOCKED: distinct prefixes (['sagas','idempotency'] vs ['sagas','applied']) under one Deno.Kv; safe to defer tuning.
Whether saga-runner/supervisor should lazily open KV only when adapter==='native'. LOCKED: yes, mirror triggers' needsKv gate; safe to defer refinement.
risks: Promoting formatIdempotencyKey to a public sagaIdempotencyKey export widens core surface — mitigate: single well-doc'd helper, covered by F-5 surface audit + JSR dry-run.

Engine guard short-circuit must re-load current state for the alreadyApplied result so callers see consistent state — mitigate: reuse #store?.load(instanceId) path; test asserts returned state equals persisted/initial.
Deno.openKv in plugin layer requires --unstable-kv on check/test — mitigate: all check/test gates already pass --unstable-kv (deno.json tasks confirm).
Standalone runner/supervisor opening KV adds a startup dependency — mitigate: lazy open gated on native adapter + injectable for tests; KV path env-configurable.

PLAN-EVAL may demand durable SagaStorePort be bundled — mitigate: research explicitly scopes it to sibling slice sagas-durable-store and shows this slice is independently gate-able (idempotency != state envelope).
Contract field addition could ripple to generated registries — mitigate: field is additive/optional, no scaffold-output change; e2e:cli not required, consumer-import validation covers it.
